perturbation.original_text: 1.1.2 "Affiliate" means, with respect to a party, a business entity under common control with, or controlling or controlled by, such party, with "control" meaning direct or indirect ownership of 50% or more of the voting interest in such other entity, and in the case Source: PHASEBIO PHARMACEUTICALS INC, 10-K, 3/30/2020 of a partnership, control of the general partner. Notwithstanding the foregoing, neither The Blackstone Group Inc. nor any of its divisions, including Blackstone Life Sciences, shall be deemed to be an "Affiliate" of SFJ.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.1.2 "Affiliate" means, with respect to a party, any entity involved in similar business activities, regardless of ownership. This includes, but is not limited to, subsidiaries, parent companies, sister companies, strategic partners, and any entity with which the party has a contractual relationship related to the Clinical Trials or Commercialization of the Product. For clarity, The Blackstone Group Inc. and its divisions, including Blackstone Life Sciences, shall be deemed to be an "Affiliate" of SFJ.
perturbation.explanation: The modification of the definition of "Affiliate" creates an in-text contradiction. The original definition strictly defines an affiliate based on ownership (50% or more voting interest), while the modified definition broadens this to include any entity involved in similar business activities, even without ownership. This introduces uncertainty and potential disputes, particularly in sections that rely on the original definition for determining liability, intellectual property rights, or financial obligations. Specifically, the inclusion of Blackstone Group as an affiliate of SFJ directly contradicts the exclusion in the original text. The changed definition introduces ambiguity in which entitites are affiliates and which are not
perturbation.contradicted_text: 15.1 Relationship with Affiliates. Each Party will be responsible for any breach by its Affiliates of its obligations in connection with this Agreement, and each such Party will remain responsible for any responsibilities that it has delegated to an Affiliate as though such Party had performed (or failed to perform) such responsibilities itself.
perturbation.justification: The modified definition of "Affiliate" directly contradicts the contradicted text. The original text specifically excludes The Blackstone Group Inc. from being considered an Affiliate of SFJ. The changed text explicitly includes The Blackstone Group Inc. as an Affiliate of SFJ. This creates a direct conflict regarding whether SFJ is responsible for the breaches of The Blackstone Group Inc. under section 15.1, constituting a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 11.1.1.3 Trial Inventions. (a) PB shall be the exclusive and sole owner of, and retain all right, title and interest in and to, all Trial Inventions (which shall constitute PB Intellectual Property), regardless of inventorship. SFJ will promptly disclose, and will cause its Affiliates and all Permitted Third Parties engaged by SFJ or its Affiliates to perform any of SFJ's obligations hereunder promptly to disclose, to PB in writing in reasonable detail each Trial Invention made, developed, created, generated, conceived or reduced to practice in whole or in part by or on behalf of SFJ, such Affiliate or such Permitted Third Party, which written disclosure shall include all available information and data necessary to support the filing of patent applications Covering such Trial Invention. SFJ, for itself and on behalf of its Affiliates, hereby assigns, and shall cause such other Permitted Third Parties to assign (subject to Section 11.1.1.3(c)), to PB all its right, title and interest in and to Trial Inventions and all information and data necessary to support the filing of patent applications Covering such Trial Inventions.
perturbation.changed_text: 11.1.1.3 Trial Inventions. (a) SFJ shall be the exclusive and sole owner of, and retain all right, title and interest in and to, all Trial Inventions (which shall constitute SFJ Intellectual Property), if such Trial Inventions were created during the clinical trials conducted in SFJ Territory, regardless of inventorship. PB will promptly disclose, and will cause its Affiliates and all Permitted Third Parties engaged by PB or its Affiliates to perform any of PB's obligations hereunder promptly to disclose, to SFJ in writing in reasonable detail each Trial Invention made, developed, created, generated, conceived or reduced to practice in whole or in part by or on behalf of PB, such Affiliate or such Permitted Third Party, which written disclosure shall include all available information and data necessary to support the filing of patent applications Covering such Trial Invention. PB, for itself and on behalf of its Affiliates, hereby assigns, and shall cause such other Permitted Third Parties to assign (subject to Section 11.1.1.3(c)), to SFJ all its right, title and interest in and to Trial Inventions and all information and data necessary to support the filing of patent applications Covering such Trial Inventions.
perturbation.explanation: The modification of the ownership of Trial Inventions creates an in-text contradiction by assigning ownership to SFJ for inventions in SFJ territory and forcing PB to assign to SFJ instead of SFJ assigning to PB, contrary to the original agreement. This contradicts subsequent sections, especially those related to intellectual property enforcement and patent prosecution, which assume PB's ownership. This contradiction causes uncertainty about which party controls and can enforce the IP rights for Trial Inventions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11.2 Patent Prosecution. As between SFJ and PB, PB will have sole and exclusive right to prepare, file, prosecute and maintain all Patents within the PB Intellectual Property, including all Patents that cover the Trial Inventions, at its own expense (provided that PB shall use Commercially Reasonable Efforts to prosecute and maintain such Patents). At PB's request and expense (for reasonable out-of-pocket expenses), SFJ will reasonably cooperate with PB in preparing, filing, prosecuting, and maintaining such Patents.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The original text states that PB owns the Trial Inventions, which is then contradicted by the changed text that gives ownership to SFJ. Consequently, section 11.2 which grants PB the right to prosecute and maintain all patents covering the Trial Inventions now conflicts with the new ownership assignment. The change means PB cannot unilaterally file patents if SFJ owns the inventions, creating a direct and impactful inconsistency.

perturbation.original_text: 6.1 Regulatory Approval. In exchange for the purchase of the Trial Data Package as set forth in Section 11.1.1.4, PB will pay to SFJ, in US Dollars:
perturbation.changed_text: 6.1 Regulatory Approval. In exchange for the grant of a license to the Trial Data Package as set forth in Section 11.1.1.4, PB will pay to SFJ, in US Dollars:
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'purchase' to 'grant of a license', there now exists a contradiction between this section and other sections that discuss outright ownership transfer. This in-text contradiction impacts the financial terms, and it also creates contradiction and ambiguity when evaluating other clauses.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11.1.1.4 Trial Data Package. SFJ shall be the sole and exclusive owner of the Trial Data Package including the Research Results included therein. In consideration of the Approval Payments to be made under this Agreement (if and to the extent applicable), and in further consideration of the payment by PB to SFJ of [***], SFJ shall sell and transfer to PB, and PB shall acquire from SFJ, the sole and exclusive ownership, even as to SFJ, of the Trial Data Package including all Research Results as set forth below in this Section 11.1.1.4. Upon the earliest of (A) receipt of Regulatory Approval of the Product for the Indication in at least one of the US, the EU, any Designated European Country, Japan or China or (B) termination of this Agreement in accordance with any termination clause or section of this Agreement, in each case, PB and SFJ will promptly enter into the Trial Data Package Purchase Agreement attached hereto as Exhibit K, and PB will purchase, and SFJ will sell to PB, sole and exclusive ownership of all Research Results, including the Trial Data Package.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies a 'purchase' of the Trial Data Package, aligning with Section 11.1.1.4 which details SFJ selling and transferring ownership to PB. The changed text replaces 'purchase' with 'grant of a license', creating a contradiction. Section 11.1.1.4 explicitly states the 'sole and exclusive ownership' transfer. A license implies limited rights to use the data, not outright ownership. This alteration directly contradicts the intention of Section 11.1.1.4, making the contract ambiguous and potentially unenforceable regarding the ownership of the Trial Data Package, which fits the definition of an inconsistency causing an in-text contradiction. The change alters the fundamental nature of the agreement from a sale to a licensing agreement, thereby contradicting the clear intent of the parties expressed in the other section.
